description: Tone color is the character of a voice independent of pitch or rhythm. It is what makes a voice feel warm or clear, heavy or light, near or distant. This specification defines five dimensions of tonal quality that synthesis models can target. Tone color is not emotion — it is the acoustic signature of how voice is produced in the body.

dimensions.name: Warmth
dimensions.description: The perceived thermal quality of voice. Warm voice has more low-frequency energy, fuller harmonics, and softer onset. Cool voice is clearer, thinner, more precise.

acoustic_correlates.cool: Higher spectral center of gravity, less low-frequency energy, crisp consonants
acoustic_correlates.neutral: Balanced spectral distribution, no emphasis on warmth or clarity
acoustic_correlates.warm: Lower spectral center, prominent fundamental and low harmonics, soft consonant onset
acoustic_correlates.deep_warm: Very low spectral center, chest resonance dominant, vowels carry more energy than consonants

dimensions.training_note: Warmth is not friendliness. A warm voice in elder-tone carries gravity. A warm voice in aramaic-liturgical carries reverence. The warmth is physical, not social.

dimensions.name: Weight
dimensions.description: The perceived gravitational quality of voice. Heavy voice feels grounded, anchored, unhurried. Light voice feels agile, effortless, floating.

acoustic_correlates.light: Higher pitch center, faster articulation, less duration on stressed syllables
acoustic_correlates.grounded: Lower pitch tendency, longer stressed syllables, deliberate articulation
acoustic_correlates.heavy: Low pitch, maximum duration on stressed syllables, slow onset, each word lands

dimensions.training_note: Weight is not volume. A whispered voice can carry enormous weight through duration and placement. Silent-echo mode is low-volume but high-weight.

dimensions.name: Brightness
dimensions.description: The perceived luminosity of voice. Bright voice cuts through, projects, arrives quickly. Dark voice recedes, enfolds, takes time to reach the listener.

acoustic_correlates.dark: Attenuated high frequencies, rounded vowels, back-of-mouth placement
acoustic_correlates.shaded: Slightly attenuated highs, soft edges on consonants
acoustic_correlates.neutral: Full spectrum, no emphasis in any direction
acoustic_correlates.bright: Prominent upper harmonics, forward placement, clear sibilants
acoustic_correlates.crystalline: Maximum high-frequency clarity, precise articulation, each phoneme distinct

dimensions.training_note: Brightness is appropriate for clarity-critical modes (blind-nav, debug-tone). Darkness is appropriate for contemplative or sacred modes (silent-echo, aramaic-liturgical). Neither is better — they serve different listening contexts.

dimensions.name: Clarity
dimensions.description: The transparency of voice — how easily each word is distinguished. Distinct from brightness: a voice can be dark and clear (low resonance, precise articulation) or bright and veiled (high energy, blurred boundaries).

acoustic_correlates.veiled: Blurred word boundaries, connected speech, vowel-dominant. Used in contemplative modes.
acoustic_correlates.soft_focus: Gentle articulation, words distinguishable but not sharp. Natural conversation.
acoustic_correlates.clear: Standard articulation, all phonemes distinguishable at normal attention.
acoustic_correlates.precise: Enhanced articulation, slight pause between words, consonants fully formed.
acoustic_correlates.hyper_articulated: Maximum phoneme distinction, each word isolated. For hearing-impaired contexts.

dimensions.training_note: Hard-of-hearing mode needs hyper_articulated clarity. Dyslexia-steady needs precise clarity with slower delivery. Hawaiian-oli needs veiled clarity — the words flow into each other as breath flows.

dimensions.name: Presence
dimensions.description: The perceived spatial distance of voice. Intimate voice feels close to the ear, as if spoken for one person. Projected voice fills a space, addressing a group or the horizon.

acoustic_correlates.distant: More reverb, softer attack, reduced high frequencies. Voice is coming from across the room.
acoustic_correlates.centered: Balanced, natural distance. Voice is in front of you at conversational range.
acoustic_correlates.near: Less reverb, warmer low end, closer proximity feel. Voice is beside you.
acoustic_correlates.intimate: Minimal reverb, full low-frequency presence, whisper-adjacent. Voice is at your ear.
acoustic_correlates.internal: Near-whisper, maximum proximity, almost inside the listener's head. For contemplative or biofeedback modes.

dimensions.training_note: Presence is not volume. A projected voice (distant/centered) can be quiet. An intimate voice can carry authority. Maori-whaikorero is centered-to-projected (marae oratory addresses the space). Silent-echo is intimate-to-internal.

compound_profiles.description: Common combinations of tone color dimensions that form recognizable vocal characters.
